Description: Cyber Security is the practice of protecting systems and networks from digital attacks.
Cyber Security offers a high-demand career protecting data and infrastructure from evolving digital threats.
A firewall is a security system that monitors and controls network traffic.
Phishing is a fraudulent attempt to obtain sensitive information by disguising as a trustworthy entity.
Encryption is the method of converting data into a coded format to prevent unauthorized access.
Ethical hacking is authorized testing to find and fix security vulnerabilities.
A Virtual Private Network (VPN) creates a secure connection over the internet.
A Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack overwhelms a system to disrupt service.
Antivirus software detects and removes malicious software from devices.
Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds an extra layer of security by requiring two forms of identification.
Social engineering manipulates individuals to disclose sensitive information.
Cryptography secures information through encoding techniques.
An IDS monitors networks for suspicious activity.
Patch management involves updating software to fix vulnerabilities.
Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment for access.
Spyware secretly collects user information without consent.
A Trojan horse disguises malware as legitimate software.
A worm is self-replicating malware that spreads across networks.
A keylogger records keystrokes to steal sensitive information.
Hashing converts data into a fixed-length string for security.
Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) encrypts data for secure online communication.
Transport Layer Security (TLS) is an updated version of SSL.
Penetration testing simulates attacks to identify vulnerabilities.
A zero-day exploit targets unknown software vulnerabilities.
A brute force attack tries multiple password combinations.
A MITM attack intercepts communication between two parties.
SQL injection inserts malicious code into database queries.
XSS injects malicious scripts into trusted websites.
A security policy outlines rules to protect organizational assets.
Biometric authentication uses physical traits like fingerprints for access.
Network segmentation divides networks to limit attack spread.
Incident response is a plan to address security breaches.
A data breach is unauthorized access to sensitive information.
The dark web is a hidden internet layer often used for illegal activities.
Endpoint security protects devices like laptops and phones.
Machine Learning is a field of AI that allows computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed.
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
Supervised learning uses labeled data to train models.
Unsupervised learning finds hidden patterns or intrinsic structures in data.
Reinforcement learning is based on agents learning by reward or punishment.
Overfitting happens when a model performs well on training data but poorly on new data.
A subset of ML using neural networks with many layers to learn from large data.
Feature engineering creates or selects relevant input variables.
Cross-validation assesses model performance on different data subsets.
Bias-variance tradeoff balances model complexity and generalization.
Gradient descent optimizes model parameters by minimizing errors.
Hyperparameter tuning adjusts settings to improve model performance.
A confusion matrix summarizes classification model performance.
Precision measures the proportion of correct positive predictions.
Recall measures the proportion of actual positives correctly identified.
F1 score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall.
Clustering groups similar data points without labels.
Dimensionality reduction simplifies data while retaining key information.
Principal Component Analysis (PCA) reduces data dimensions.
Regression predicts continuous numerical outcomes.
Classification predicts categorical outcomes.
Ensemble learning combines multiple models for better results.
Bagging reduces variance by training models on random data subsets.
Boosting improves weak models by focusing on errors.
Regularization prevents overfitting by penalizing complexity.
Decision Trees split data based on feature conditions.
Random Forest is an ensemble of decision trees.
Support Vector Machine (SVM) finds the best hyperplane for classification.
K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifies based on closest data points.
Linear Regression models relationships between variables.
Logistic Regression predicts binary outcomes.
Neural Networks mimic human brain for complex pattern recognition.
A* (A Star) is a pathfinding algorithm using heuristics for optimal routes.
AO* is a heuristic search algorithm for AND-OR graphs.
Naive Bayes uses probability for classification tasks.
Gradient Boosting builds models sequentially to minimize errors.
XGBoost is an optimized gradient boosting algorithm.
LightGBM is a fast gradient boosting framework.
CatBoost handles categorical features efficiently.
K-Means clusters data into K groups based on similarity.
DBSCAN clusters data based on density.
Hierarchical clustering builds a tree of clusters.
Apriori finds frequent itemsets in transactional data.
MDP models decision-making in reinforcement learning.
Q-Learning is a model-free reinforcement learning algorithm.
Data Science is an interdisciplinary field focusing on extracting knowledge and insights from data.
Data wrangling is the process of cleaning and transforming raw data into usable format.
A data pipeline automates the flow of data from source to analysis.
Exploratory Data Analysis summarizes data characteristics through visualization.
Big data involves massive datasets requiring distributed storage and processing.
Data mining involves finding patterns and relationships in large datasets.
Data visualization helps understand data trends through charts and graphs.
Hadoop is a framework for distributed storage and processing.
Apache Spark is a fast engine for big data processing.
SQL is a language for querying and managing databases.
NoSQL databases handle unstructured or semi-structured data.
A data warehouse stores large amounts of historical data.
Pandas is a Python library for data manipulation.
NumPy is a Python library for numerical computations.
Matplotlib is a Python library for data visualization.
Seaborn is a Python library for statistical visualizations.
Power BI is a Microsoft tool for interactive data visualization.
Tableau is a platform for creating interactive dashboards.
Statistical analysis uses math to interpret data.
Hypothesis testing evaluates assumptions about data.
P-value measures the strength of evidence against a hypothesis.
Correlation measures the relationship between variables.
Regression analysis models relationships between variables.
Time series analysis studies data points over time.
Data cleaning removes errors and inconsistencies.
Missing data is handled by imputation or removal.
Outlier detection identifies unusual data points.
Data normalization scales data to a standard range.
Feature selection chooses relevant variables for analysis.
Data ethics ensures responsible and fair data use.
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the simulation of human intelligence in machines to perform tasks like reasoning and learning.
AI began in the 1950s with pioneers like Alan Turing; milestones include expert systems and deep learning advancements.
AI is the broad concept of intelligent machines; ML is a subset where systems learn from data; DL uses deep neural networks.
Depth Limited Search restricts DFS to a depth limit to avoid infinite loops.
Uniform Cost Search finds the lowest-cost path by exploring nodes with the least total cost first.
Iterative Deepening DFS combines BFS's completeness with DFS's low memory use by increasing depth limits.
Beam Search optimizes Best First Search by limiting the search to top-k promising nodes.
A* (A Star) uses heuristics plus path cost to find the optimal path efficiently.
State Space Search models problems like the Eight Tile Puzzle as states and transitions.
Knowledge Representation (KR) organizes information for AI reasoning and decision-making.
Propositional Logic uses true/false statements with syntax and inference rules for reasoning.
First-Order Logic (FOL) uses predicates and quantifiers to represent complex relationships.
Forward chaining starts with facts and applies rules to reach conclusions.
Backward chaining starts with goals and works backward to find supporting facts.
An inference engine applies rules to derive conclusions in expert systems.
Knowledge acquisition gathers expert knowledge for AI systems.
Rule-based systems use predefined rules; case-based systems reason from past examples.
Semantic networks represent knowledge as nodes (concepts) and edges (relationships).
Frames organize knowledge into structured attributes for reasoning.
Scripts represent stereotypical event sequences for contextual understanding.
Bayes Theorem calculates conditional probabilities for uncertain reasoning.
Bayesian Belief Networks model probabilistic relationships among variables.
Adversarial search models competitive environments like games.
Minimax algorithm evaluates game moves to minimize loss and maximize gain.
Alpha-Beta Pruning optimizes Minimax by eliminating unpromising branches.
Linear Regression models linear relationships between variables for prediction.
Logistic Regression predicts binary outcomes using a sigmoid function.
Hyperparameter tuning optimizes model settings for better performance.
Decision Trees split data based on feature conditions for classification or regression.
Random Forests combine multiple decision trees for improved accuracy.
SVMs find the optimal hyperplane to separate data classes.
K-Means Clustering groups data into K clusters based on similarity.
ANNs mimic human brains with interconnected nodes for learning.
MLP is a feedforward ANN with multiple layers and backpropagation.
Transfer Learning reuses pre-trained models for new tasks.
